signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live MON short for now. It is still profitable, retaining about two-thirds of peak P&L, and the warning is only at warning level rather than urgent.
BTC is strong, but there is no compelling edge here and the caution regime keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode.
ETH is constructive but not enough to justify fresh risk while the event brake is still in caution mode.
SOL remains one of the better long candidates because mechanics are bullish and funding is still negative enough to support a squeeze-style continuation, though caution keeps conviction capped.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is extremely positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks fragile on the upside.
MAVIA also screens as a fade candidate because funding is strongly positive and the market quality is weak enough that upside continuation is unreliable.
HEMI has the same basic fade profile as STBL and MAVIA: positive funding, crowded positioning, and weak enough structure to short rather than chase.
HMSTR is too illiquid and noisy to be a serious setup despite mild momentum.
PURR is another thin market with too little quality to justify action.
BANANA is a valid squeeze-style long because mechanics are strong and funding is deeply negative, but caution and thin liquidity keep it to reduced-confidence status.
DASH remains a decent long candidate because the board is bullish and funding is supportive for continuation, though the caution regime keeps it below full conviction.
